description: Figure 6 presents the functional relationships between characteristic rheological stress curve values and the Zener-Hollomon parameter (expressed as lnZ) for a low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel under hot deformation. The three subplots display double-Y-axis scatter plots with regression fits obtained via multiple linear regression: (a) critical stress σ_c in MPa (black stars, R = 0.947) and critical strain ε_c (red circles, R = 0.911); (b) peak stress σ_p in MPa (black stars, R = 0.957) and peak strain ε_p (red circles, R = 0.911); and (c) steady-state stress σ_s in MPa (black stars, R = 0.956) and steady-state strain ε_s (red circles, R = 0.922). All datasets are plotted against lnZ on the x-axis. Both characteristic stresses and strains monotonically increase with increasing lnZ, reflecting higher flow resistance and greater strain accumulation under lower temperatures and higher strain rates. The stress curves exhibit nonlinear upward trends, while the strain dependencies are approximately linear. These strong correlations demonstrate that the Z parameter successfully unifies the thermal and mechanical processing variables, enabling constitutive modeling of dynamic recrystallization kinetics for this steel.
